sources: Polish Ministry of Finance: Budget Act 2024 (Ustawa budżetowa na rok 2024)
Polish Ministry of National Defense: Budget 2024
meta.notes: UBI figures assume 50% of savings distributed equally to all 38M Polish residents. All figures converted from PLN at ~4 PLN/USD. Mandatory program figures cover social insurance and health fund contributions; discretionary figures are enacted appropriations.

items.name: ZUS — Social Insurance Institution (Zakład Ubezpieczeń Społecznych)
items.description: Poland's national social insurance system covering retirement pensions, disability benefits, sickness pay, and maternity benefits for ~15M insured workers and pensioners.
items.cut_note: Directly supports ~15M pension recipients and millions of workers who paid into the system throughout their careers.

items.name: NFZ — National Health Fund (Narodowy Fundusz Zdrowia)
items.description: Poland's public health insurance system funding hospital care, outpatient services, prescription drug reimbursement, and specialist treatment for all insured residents.
items.cut_note: Funds universal healthcare for 38M Poles — cutting would eliminate basic medical coverage for the entire population.

items.name: Ministry of National Defense (Ministerstwo Obrony Narodowej)
items.description: Polish Armed Forces including army, navy, air force, and territorial defense. Poland has committed to 4% of GDP on defense due to its NATO eastern flank position bordering Russia and Belarus.

items.name: Ministry of Education (Ministerstwo Edukacji Narodowej)
items.description: Public primary and secondary education including teacher salaries, school subsidies, curriculum development, and special needs education for ~4.5M students.

items.name: Local Government Transfers (Subwencje i dotacje dla samorządów)
items.description: Equalization grants and general-purpose subsidies transferred from the central government to Poland's 16 voivodeships, 314 counties, and 2,477 municipalities.

items.name: Infrastructure & Road Fund (Fundusz Dróg Samorządowych / CPK)
items.description: National road construction and maintenance (GDDKiA), railway investment (PKP PLK), and Central Communication Port (CPK) hub airport and rail network project.

items.name: Debt Interest Service (Obsługa długu publicznego)
items.description: Interest payments on Poland's public debt (~$280B / ~55% of GDP). Payments on treasury bonds and EU/IMF loans that come due regardless of budget priorities.
items.cut_note: Defaulting on sovereign debt would trigger credit rating collapse and cut Poland off from international capital markets.

items.name: Social Welfare Programs (500+/800+, Family Benefits)
items.agency: Ministry of Family and Social Policy
items.description: Poland's flagship child benefit program (800 PLN/month per child under 18), family care allowances, social assistance, and poverty relief programs benefiting ~6M families.
items.cut_note: The 800+ child benefit is Poland's most popular social program, credited with a major reduction in child poverty.

items.name: Agriculture & Rural Development (Ministerstwo Rolnictwa)
items.agency: Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development
items.description: National co-financing of EU Common Agricultural Policy (CAP) direct payments, rural development programs, agricultural advisory services, and food safety inspection (IJHARS).

items.name: Science & Higher Education (MEiN — universities and R&D)
items.agency: Ministry of Science and Higher Education
items.description: Subsidies for Poland's ~130 public universities, national research grants (NCN, NCBR), and Polish Academy of Sciences research institutes serving ~1.2M students.

items.name: Police & Internal Security (Policja, ABW, Straż Graniczna)
items.agency: Ministry of Internal Affairs and Administration
items.description: National Police (100,000+ officers), Internal Security Agency (ABW counterintelligence), Border Guard, and Government Protection Bureau (BOR) protecting senior officials.
